steps.title: State the no-solution condition
steps.content:
Rearrange $6x+4=rx+9$ by collecting $x$ terms:
$$6x-rx=9-4$$
$$(6-r)x=5$$
For no solution, the coefficient of $x$ must be $0$, producing the false statement $0=5$. That means $6-r=0$, so $r=6$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $36$: $(6-36)x = 5 \Rightarrow -30x = 5 \Rightarrow x = -\frac{1}{6}$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice B — $10$: $(6-10)x = 5 \Rightarrow -4x = 5 \Rightarrow x = -\frac{5}{4}$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice C — $9$: $(6-9)x = 5 \Rightarrow -3x = 5 \Rightarrow x = -\frac{5}{3}$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice D — $6$: $(6-6)x = 5 \Rightarrow 0 = 5$. Contradiction — no solution exists. ✓
That matches Choice D — $6$.
